rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical aryl radical cyclization-carboxylation (ERCC) of 2-allyloxybromobenzenes with CO2 using methyl 4-tert-butylbenzoate as an electron-transfer mediator and a sacrificial Mg anode to give 2,3-dihydrobenzofuran-3-ylacetic acids
electrochemical_role: Cathodic one-electron reduction of methyl 4-tert-butylbenzoate (4) at Pt cathode generates its radical anion, which selectively transfers one electron to the aryl bromide to produce an aryl radical (avoiding two-electron reduction to the aryl anion); Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to Mg2+

key_intermediates.name: radical anion of methyl 4-tert-butylbenzoate (mediator 4 reduced form)
aryl radical from 2-allyloxybromobenzene after Br- loss
aryl anion (off-pathway, direct 2e- reduction without mediator)

electron_flow: At the Pt cathode, mediator 4 is reduced to its radical anion, which transfers one electron to the aryl bromide to expel Br- and form an aryl radical; the aryl radical undergoes 5-exo-trig cyclization onto the pendant allyl group, the resulting alkyl radical is further reduced at the cathode to a carbanion, which is trapped by CO2 to give a carboxylate (stabilized by Mg2+ from the sacrificial Mg anode).
rate_determining_step: mediated single-electron transfer from the radical anion of 4 to the aryl bromide (generation of aryl radical)

probe_reagents.name: methyl 4-tert-butylbenzoate (mediator 4)
probe_reagents.observation: CV alone in DMF shows reversible reduction at -2.9 V vs Ag/Ag+; addition of 0.5 equiv 1a increases reduction peak current and decreases oxidation peak; with 1.0 equiv 1a the oxidation peak disappears, consistent with catalytic mediator behavior generating aryl radicals from ArBr

probe_reagents.name: 2-allyloxybromobenzene (1a)
probe_reagents.observation: Irreversible reduction at -3.2 V vs Ag/Ag+ in DMF (direct reduction much harder than mediated pathway)

notes: Without mediator 4, direct two-electron cathodic reduction of ArBr competes to give an aryl anion that is carboxylated before cyclization, leading to benzoic acid byproducts. With alkyne substrate 15, β-selective overcarboxylation of the initially formed α,β-unsaturated acid gives dicarboxylic acid 16 as the sole product. The Mg sacrificial anode is essential to prevent anodic oxidation of carbanion/carboxylate intermediates and to stabilize them as Mg carboxylates.
